html:
COMMISSION IMPLEMENTING REGULATION (EU) 2026/1421

amending Implementing Regulation (EU) No 540/2011 as regards the extension of approval periods for the active substances bensulfuron-methyl, benzovindiflupyr, chlorotoluron, clodinafop-propargyl, cyprodinil, cymoxanil, dazomet, deltamethrin, diclofop-methyl, fenazaquin, fluopicolide, hymexazol, lambda-cyhalothrin, MCPA, MCPB, metaldehyde, metsulfuron-methyl, paclobutrazol, and tebuconazole.

THE EUROPEAN COMMISSION HAS ADOPTED THIS REGULATION,

having regard to the Treaty on the Functioning of the European Union,

having regard to Regulation (EC) No 1107/2009 of the European Parliament and of the Council of 21 October 2009 concerning the placing of plant protection products on the market and amending or replacing certain Council Directives ( 1 ), in particular Article 17(1), and

whereas the active substances bensulfuron-methyl, benzovindiflupyr, chlorotoluron, clodinafop-propargyl, cyprodinil, cymoxanil, dazomet, deltamethrin, diclofop-methyl, fenazaquin, fluopicolide, hymexazol, lambda-cyhalothrin, MCPA, MCPB, metaldehyde, metsulfuron-methyl, paclobutrazol, and tebuconazole were approved as active substances for use in plant protection products for a limited period, and the applications for their renewal are currently being assessed pursuant to Article 14 of Regulation (EC) No 1107/2009;

whereas Commission Directive 2000/49/EC ( 2 ) included metsulfuron-methyl in Annex I to Council Directive 91/414/EEC ( 3 ) until 30 June 2011; whereas the approval of that active substance was renewed as a candidate for substitution by Commission Implementing Regulation (EU) 2016/139 ( 4 ) until 31 March 2023, and it was included in Part E of the Annex to Commission Implementing Regulation (EU) No 540/2011 ( 5 ); whereas the approval of metsulfuron-methyl has been extended twice, most recently by Commission Implementing Regulation (EU) 2024/324 ( 6 ) until 31 August 2026.

According to the European Commission's Directive 2000/80/EC (7), lambda-cyhalothrin was included as an active substance in Annex I of Directive 91/414/EEC until December 31, 2011. The European Commission's Implementing Regulation (EU) 2016/146 (8) renewed the approval of this active substance as a candidate for substitution until March 31, 2023, and it was included in Part E of the Annex to Implementing Regulation (EU) No 540/2011. The approval of lambda-cyhalothrin has been extended twice, most recently by the European Commission's Implementing Regulation (EU) 2024/324 until August 31, 2026.

According to the European Commission's Directive 2003/5/EC (9), deltamethrin was included as an active substance in Annex I of Directive 91/414/EEC until October 31, 2013, and subsequently it was included in Part A of the Annex to Implementing Regulation (EU) No 540/2011. The approval of deltamethrin has been extended nine times, most recently by the European Commission's Implementing Regulation (EU) 2023/1757 (10) until August 15, 2026.

According to the European Commission's Directive 2005/53/EC (11), chlorotoluron was included as an active substance in Annex I of Directive 91/414/EEC until February 28, 2016, and subsequently it was included in Part A of the Annex to Implementing Regulation (EU) No 540/2011. The approval of chlorotoluron has been extended eight times, most recently by Implementing Regulation (EU) 2023/1757 until August 15, 2026.

According to the European Commission's Directive 2005/57/EC (12), MCPA and MCPB were included as active substances in Annex I of Directive 91/414/EEC until April 30, 2016, and subsequently they were included in Part A of the Annex to Implementing Regulation (EU) No 540/2011. The approvals of MCPA and MCPB have been extended eight times, most recently by Implementing Regulation (EU) 2023/1757 until August 15, 2026.

According to the Commission's Directive 2008/125/EC (13), cymoxanil and tebukonazole were included as active substances in Annex I of Directive 91/414/EEC until August 31, 2019, and subsequently added to Part A of the annex to Implementing Regulation (EU) No 540/2011. The approvals for cymoxanil and tebukonazole have been extended four and five times respectively, most recently by the Commission's Implementing Regulation (EU) 2023/1446 (14), until August 15, 2026.

According to the Commission's Directive 2009/11/EC (15), bensulfuron was included as an active substance in Annex I of Directive 91/414/EEC until October 31, 2019, and subsequently added to Part A of the annex to Implementing Regulation (EU) No 540/2011. The approval for bensulfuron has been extended three times, most recently by Implementing Regulation (EU) 2023/1757, until August 15, 2026.

According to the Commission's Directive 2010/15/EU (16), fluopicolide was included as an active substance in Annex I of Directive 91/414/EEC until May 31, 2020, and subsequently added to Part A of the annex to Implementing Regulation (EU) No 540/2011. The approval for fluopicolide has been extended twice, most recently by the Commission's Implementing Regulation (EU) 2023/918 (17), until August 31, 2026.

According to the Commission's Directive 2011/4/EU (18), cyprodinil was included as an active substance in Annex I of Directive 91/414/EEC until May 31, 2021, and subsequently added to Part A of the annex to Implementing Regulation (EU) No 540/2011. The approval for cyprodinil has been extended twice, most recently by Implementing Regulation (EU) 2023/918, until August 31, 2026.

According to the European Commission's Directive 2011/5/EU (19), hymexazol was included as an active substance in Annex I of Directive 91/414/EEC until May 31, 2021, and subsequently added to Part A of the annex to Implementing Regulation (EU) No 540/2011. The approval of hymexazol has been extended twice, most recently by Implementing Regulation (EU) 2023/918 until August 31, 2026.

According to the European Commission's Directive 2011/21/EU (20), kletodim was included as an active substance in Annex I of Directive 91/414/EEC until May 31, 2021, and subsequently added to Part A of the annex to Implementing Regulation (EU) No 540/2011. The approval of kletodim has been extended twice, most recently by Implementing Regulation (EU) 2023/918 until August 31, 2026.

According to the European Commission's Implementing Directive 2011/39/EU (21), fenazakin was included as an active substance in Annex I of Directive 91/414/EEC until May 31, 2021, and subsequently added to Part A of the annex to Implementing Regulation (EU) No 540/2011. The approval of fenazakin has been extended twice, most recently by Implementing Regulation (EU) 2023/918 until August 31, 2026.

According to the European Commission's Implementing Directive 2011/45/EU (22), diclofop was included as an active substance in Annex I of Directive 91/414/EEC until May 31, 2021, and subsequently added to Part A of the annex to Implementing Regulation (EU) No 540/2011. The approval of diclofop has been extended twice, most recently by Implementing Regulation (EU) 2023/918 until August 31, 2026.

According to the European Commission's Implementing Directive 2011/53/EU (23), dazomet was included as an active substance in Annex I of Directive 91/414/EEC until May 31, 2021, and subsequently added to
tldr: The European Parliament has extended the period during which several chemicals can be used in plant protection products (which control weeds or diseases in plants). This extension is being granted while authorities evaluate whether these substances should continue to be allowed on the market.
lattlast:
The European Parliament has decided to extend the approval for a long list of chemical substances used in plant protection products. Plant protection products are items that farmers and gardeners use to protect crops from pests, fungi, or weeds.

Because it takes time for authorities to investigate whether these substances are safe for the environment and human health, they have chosen to extend the period during which they can be sold on the market. For most of the substances on the list, such as deltamethrin and chlorotoluron, the new deadline extends until August 2026.

Certain specific substances, such as methosulam and lambda-cyhalothrin, have had their approvals extended until August 2026. Other substances, such as fluopicolide and cyprodinil, have also been granted more time for evaluation. This decision ensures that the products can continue to be used while the new assessments are carried out.
